text: @GovofCO Permanent daylight saving time doesn’t stop the clock shift, it just makes schools and businesses do the math. Instead of changing our clocks twice a year, we’ll just end up changing school start times and work hours to avoid a 8:30 AM winter sunrise.
fullText: @GovofCO Permanent daylight saving time doesn’t stop the clock shift, it just makes schools and businesses do the math. Instead of changing our clocks twice a year, we’ll just end up changing school start times and work hours to avoid a 8:30 AM winter sunrise.

text:
Reminder that "Daylight Saving Time" has no S. It's not "Savings."

I will not be taking questions on why this took me until paragraph four to figure out.

👇

text:
We are ready to stop moving our clocks around twice each year in Colorado! Changing the clocks twice a year can be confusing and disrupt our sleep cycles. That’s why I signed a law called "Daylight Saving Time Year Round," HB22-1297, to keep Colorado in daylight saving time. This law can only take effect if the federal government and four other states in our time zone agree to do the same.

This week, the U.S. House passed a bill to make daylight saving time permanent. It now moves on to the Senate. 

https://t.co/Zhiljy3HCh
